summary:
The primary source of amniotic fluid after mid-pregnancy is fetal urination; however, early contributions come from maternal fluids as well. Agent1 reasoned based on multiple studies indicating a shift towards fetal-derived fluid starting around second-trimester development for most of the volume present late-term during gestation. Key point highlighted was transition phase where fetal role becomes predominant producer beyond certain stage due to increased renal function enabling significant amounts of urine excretion into amniotic cavity.

formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 1 summarized): The primary source of amniotic fluid after mid-pregnancy is fetal urination; however, early contributions come from maternal fluids as well. Agent1 reasoned based on multiple studies indicating a shift towards fetal-derived fluid starting around second-trimester development for most of the volume present late-term during gestation. Key point highlighted was transition phase where fetal role becomes predominant producer beyond certain stage due to increased renal function enabling significant amounts of urine excretion into amniotic cavity.
